title: [$250] Assign Card silently fails for domain-level card feeds (e.g., Chase) due to missing adminAccess Onyx key

labels.name: Reviewing
labels.description: Has a PR in review

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
## Problem When a workspace admin navigates to **Workspace > Company Cards** and attempts to assign a card from a **domain-level card feed** (e.g., Chase `oauth.chase.com`), clicking "Assign Card" on an unassigned card row **silently does nothing**. The user sees no error message, no loading indicator — the UI simply does not respond. The user gets stuck and cannot complete card assignments. This does **not** affect workspace-level card feeds (e.g., Expensify Cards), only domain-level feeds like Chase OAuth. ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Set up a workspace with a Chase commercial card feed (`oauth.chase.com`) connected at the domain level 2. Ensure there are unassigned cards in the feed 3. Navigate directly to **Workspace > Company Cards** (do NOT visit the Domain settings page first) 4. Click on an unassigned card row to start the "Assign Card" flow 5. Observe that clicking the card either does nothing, or the assign card wizard gets stuck and the user cannot complete the assignment ## Expected Behavior The assign card wizard should open and allow the admin to select an assignee, choose the card, set a transaction start date, and complete the card assignment. ## Actual Behavior Clicking "Assign Card" silently does nothing, or the wizard gets stuck on an intermediate step. No error message is shown. The backend API calls (`OpenPolicyCompanyCardsPage`, `OpenPolicyCompanyCardsFeed`) all return 200 OK — the issue is entirely on the frontend. ## Root Cause Analysis The `useAssignCard` hook in [`src/hooks/useAssignCard.ts`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/main/src/hooks/useAssignCard.ts) has a guard called `isAssigningCardDisabled` that silently returns (no-op) when any of its conditions are true: ```ts const isAssigningCardDisabled = !currentFeedData || !!currentFeedData?.pending || isSelectedFeedConnectionBroken || !isAllowedToIssueCompanyCard; ``` For **domain-level feeds** (where `domainID != workspaceAccountID`), the `useIsAllowedToIssueCompanyCard` hook in [`src/hooks/useIsAllowedToIssueCompanyCard.ts`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/main/src/hooks/useIsAllowedToIssueCompanyCard.ts) depends on the `SHARED_NVP_PRIVATE_ADMIN_ACCESS_{domainID}` Onyx key: ```ts const [adminAccess] = useOnyx(`${ONYXKEYS.COLLECTION.SHARED_NVP_PRIVATE_ADMIN_ACCESS}${selectedFeedData?.domainID}`); if (selectedFeedData?.domainID === policy?.workspaceAccountID) { return isPolicyAdmin; // workspace-level feeds — works fine } return !!adminAccess; // domain-level feeds — depends on adminAccess being populated ``` **The problem:** The `SHARED_NVP_PRIVATE_ADMIN_ACCESS` Onyx key is **only populated when the user visits the Domain settings page**. Neither `OpenPolicyCompanyCardsPage` nor `OpenPolicyCompanyCardsFeed` include this Onyx update. So if an admin navigates directly to Workspace > Company Cards without first visiting the Domain page, `adminAccess` is `undefined`, causing `isAllowedToIssueCompanyCard` to return `false`, and the assign card action silently does nothing. ## Suggested Fix **Option A (preferred):** Update the `OpenPolicyCompanyCardsPage` or `OpenPolicyCompanyCardsFeed` backend command to include the `SHARED_NVP_PRIVATE_ADMIN_ACCESS` Onyx update for domain-level feeds, so the admin access flag is available when the Company Cards page loads. **Option B:** Show an error message or visual feedback when `isAssigningCardDisabled` is true, instead of silently returning. This would at least inform the user that something is wrong. ## Workaround Navigate to **Settings > Domains > [domain name]** first, then return to **Workspace > Company Cards** and retry the card assignment. Visiting the Domain page populates the missing `adminAccess` Onyx key. ## Platform Web (Chrome), but likely affects all platforms using New Expensify.
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @situchan
Upwork Automation - Do Not Edit
  • Upwork Job URL: https://www.upwork.com/jobs/~022036541104751695205
  • Upwork Job ID: 2036541104751695205
  • Last Price Increase: 2026-03-24

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** v9.3.43-0 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** Yes **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://test-management.browserstack.com/projects/2219752/test-runs/TR-12779/folder/13176922/41237512/1276031929?sort=%2Bidentifier **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** n/a **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Regression TC Execution **Device used:** Mac 15.4.1 Safari/Chrome, iPhone 13 iOS 26.3.1 **App Component:** Search ## Action Performed: 1. Go to https://staging.new.expensify.com 2. Navigate to a conversation 3. Submit a expense with the merchant set to “Coffee shop” 4. Navigate to another conversation 5. Click on the Search icon on the header of the conversation (should be top right) 6. Type “merchant:coffee” and enter ## Expected Result: Expenses matching the entered keyword are displayed ## Actual Result: Multi-word merchants are not searchable using keywords ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [x] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/53f3dd3e-c884-4b68-b234-b60f90cc1675 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @ikevin127

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.43-0 **Reproducible in staging?:** Y **Reproducible in production?:** Y **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** **Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856 **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** **Issue reported by:** @timothysmall **Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): [#Expensify Bugs](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1774286881123369) ## Action Performed: 1. Create an expense using multi level tags 2. Go to Expenses > Filters 3. Filter by tag (South) 4. View expenses visible 5. Shows expenses also containing the "California" parent tag ## Expected Result: To only see expenses containing the "South" tag ## Actual Result: Shows expenses also containing the "California" parent tag ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7e7e0778-44cf-4549-a283-ff6c9ce7ab1a [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)
